Lilly’s Foundayo reaches 1,390 patients in first week, trailing Novo’s oral Wegovy launch
snippet: While Novo Nordisk’s Wegovy pill reached more than 3,000 patients in its first week on the market, analysts at RBC Capital Markets said a direct comparison of the two figures could be misleading given the shorter data collection time for Foundayo.

title: UPDATE: Kailera breaks biotech IPO record with $625M debut
snippet: Obesity-focused Kailera Therapeutics debuted on the Nasdaq Friday after raising a record $625 million, beating Moderna’s $600 million from 2018.

title: RFK Jr. defends Makary, claims pharma ‘owns’ Congress and media
snippet: The pharma industry “own Congress, they own the media,” Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. told lawmakers by way of explaining the bad press against FDA Commissioner Marty Makary following the second rejection of Replimune’s advanced melanoma drug.

title: Novo may have muscle advantage over Lilly in weight-loss race: preprint
snippet: More patients on Eli Lilly’s tirzepatide lost over 5% of their lean mass versus those on Novo Nordisk’s semaglutide, according to a study that has yet to be peer reviewed.

title: Replimune cries foul on regulatory flexibility. But many Americans want a stricter FDA
snippet: After Replimune’s advanced melanoma drug was rejected for a second time, CEO Sushil Patel slammed the FDA for failing to exercise regulatory flexibility, while other experts bemoaned the agency’s lack of consistency. With new safety guidelines for gene editing therapies, the FDA has taken a first step toward fixing bo...

title: Merck’s PD-1/VEGF data star in stacked lineup of AACR ‘26 data reveals
snippet: The Merck update, which will shed light on a $588 million bet to succeed Keytruda, is part of a roster of presentations that could shape the future of ADCs, protein degraders and KRAS-targeted therapies.

title: Funding the future of European biotech
snippet: In this episode of Denatured, you'll be hearing from Edoardo Negroni, co-founder & managing partner at AurorA-TT and Naveed Siddiqi, senior partner, Venture Investments at Novo Holdings. We debate whether Europe’s world-class science can be matched by a truly integrated venture ecosystem—and what it would take, in pra...

title: Trump nominates new CDC director, appoints various leaders to embattled agency
snippet: Erica Schwartz, a former deputy surgeon general and member of the U.S. military, will take over from what has amounted to a rapid succession of CDC leaders over the past year.

title: Drug pricing watchdog calls for increased transparency into FDA’s accelerated approval decisions
snippet: Greater transparency in FDA decision-making was possibly the most common theme for stakeholders interviewed by the Institute for Clinical and Economic Review in a new report focused on strengthening the agency’s accelerated approval program.

title: FDA mulls compounding for peptides previously flagged over safety risks
snippet: With Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. declaring himself “a big fan of peptides,” the FDA has arranged an advisory committee to discuss allowing compounders to make molecules despite earlier safety concerns.

title: MeiraGTx, spying hope in a failed trial, buys back eye disease gene therapy from J&J
snippet: J&J will hand over the rights to bota-vec for $25 million upfront, clearing MeiraGTx to seek regulatory approvals in the U.S. and EU in 2027.

title: Lilly’s Foundayo posts ‘outstanding’ diabetes data, possibly easing FDA safety concerns
snippet: Right after the FDA flagged safety concerns for Eli Lilly’s newly approved obesity pill, the pharma posted diabetes data demonstrating a clean safety profile and revealed plans to seek approval in the indication.

title: Can Europe turn world-class science into world-class scale?
snippet: Despite exceptional regional hubs and research strength, investors say Europe still needs more integrated incubators, smarter regulation and broader pools of patient capital to keep breakthrough companies growing at home.

title: Obesity’s explosive growth continues as Q1 deals exceed total 2025 value
snippet: Commitments in obesity- and diabetes-focused deals reached $22 billion in the first quarter of 2026—already eclipsing last year’s total of $20.3 billion, according to a new J.P. Morgan report.

title: Revolution doubles stock offering target to $2B following pancreatic cancer win
snippet: After Revolution Medicines’ drug candidate doubled survival in a Phase 3 pancreatic cancer study, the biotech is hoping to attract more investor money, with new plans for a $2 billion stock and debt offering.

title: Novartis CEO joins Anthropic board, embedding AI in the heart of biopharma
snippet: In recent months, Anthropic has been building more and more ties with the biopharma industry, including partnerships with Big Pharma companies such as Sanofi, Novo Nordisk and AbbVie.

title: Biopharmas pull back on layoffs in Q1
snippet: The trend of fewer companies letting employees go, which began in the fourth quarter of last year, has continued. However, more employees were on the chopping block in Q1 2026 than in Q1 2025, due mainly to one company’s cuts.

title: Astellas’ consolidation efforts to impact 50 Universal Cells employees
snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

title: Whitepaper: CDMOs at a Crossroads
snippet: In this whitepaper, BioSpace reviews the major trends impacting the CDMO sector and the evolving relationship between sponsors and providers. We examine the key qualities pharma and biotech should consider in CDMO selection, and how the macroeconomic and macrodevelopment factors affecting the space play a role in this...

title: BMS makes a Beeline, bringing 5 assets to biotech's $300M precision immunology debut
snippet: Leading Beeline Medicines’ pipeline is afimetoran, which is in Phase 2 development for systemic lupus erythematosus with data expected later this year.

With new safety guidelines for gene editing therapies, the FDA has taken a first step toward fixing bo...

title: With oral obesity race underway, all eyes are on Lilly’s Foundayo launch
snippet: Novo Nordisk’s oral Wegovy has a few months’ head start on Eli Lilly’s newly approved pill. While the Indianapolis pharma has come from behind the Danish rival in the weight loss space before, last time it clearly had the better drug.

title: Pharma's Reluctance To Serve Europe To Dodge MFN Drug Pricing Is Chilling
snippet: As Big Pharma companies consider foregoing European drug launches to avoid reducing drug prices in the U.S. in alignment with Trump’s Most Favored Nation policy, patients will suffer.

title: Prasad’s Request To Remain Anonymous Shines Light on FDA’s Transparency Problem
snippet: While requests by government officials for anonymity when speaking to the media are nothing new, the practice attracts more scrutiny when the Department for Health and Human Services has pledged a commitment to “radical transparency.”

title: Spatiotemporal immune dynamics in lung cancer progression and treatment.
snippet: The lung tumour microenvironment is a complex and evolving ecosystem in which immune, stromal and malignant cells interact across both space and time. Spatial organisation determines whether immune cells can access and induce immune responses across tumour tissues, while temporal changes driven by disease progression and therapy reshape these interactions.....

title: Spatial transcriptomics reveals a molecular tumor budding signature in head and neck cancer.
Research on machine learning-based clinical prediction models: a bibliometric analysis.
snippet: Machine learning (ML) has emerged as a transformative approach for developing high-performance clinical prediction models (CPMs). By leveraging multidimensional patient data, ML enables more accurate disease risk stratification, prognostic assessment, and clinical decision-making. In recent years, research on CPMs has expanded rapidly, with nearly 250,000 p...

title: Non-small cell lung cancer research: advances and persistent challenges.
snippet: Non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) remains a leading cause of cancer-related mortality, prompting significant advancements in therapeutic and precision medicine. Recent innovations include antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs) such as TROP-2-targeting agents and HER3-DXd, which show promising efficacy in refractory disease. Next-generation tyrosine kinase inhibit...

title: : A multifunctional regulator in the immune microenvironment (Review).
snippet: The Fc fragment of IgE receptor 1G () gene encodes the Fc receptor common γ chain (FcRγ), a crucial adaptor protein in immune signaling. As the core subunit for multiple immune receptors, including high-affinity immunoglobulin E receptor, Fc fragment of IgG receptor and C-type lectin receptors, it regulates both innate and adaptive immune respon...

title: Targeted therapies in pediatric oncology: A start.
snippet: The treatment of cancers was revolutionized by the use of combination "conventional" chemotherapy, first in children, during the 20century. That foundation of therapy, while having achieved significant advances in survival, has reached its limits. Innovations in targeted therapies are now building upon that foundation to further improve outcomes in pediatri...

title: A prospective observational study on the value of HPV E6/E7 oncoprotein immunocytochemistry for triaging HPV-positive women.
snippet: While human papillomavirus testing has become the primary screening modality for cervical cancer prevention, its implementation necessitates supplementary triage strategies. The viral oncoproteins E6 and E7, serving as primary mediators of high-risk HPV (hrHPV)-induced carcinogenesis, emerge as viable candidates for this risk stratification. We aimed to exa...

title: Combining label-free Raman spectroscopy and machine learning to identify early biomarkers of COVID-19 disease severity and mortality.
snippet: Early prediction of COVID-19 severity and mortality is crucial for optimizing clinical care and patient outcomes, but remains challenging. We aim to develop a screening tool combining label-free Raman spectroscopy and machine learning modeling to predict COVID-19 severity and mortality. Patients infected by SARS-CoV-2 () were recruited during the first wave...

title: Redefining standards: a comprehensive systematic review of practice changing advances in GU oncology from ASCO and ESMO 2025.
snippet: The year 2025 has been a transformative moment in GU oncology, with paradigm-shifting clinical trial results presented at major conferences and rapidly published. These developments are recasting therapeutic standards across bladder, kidney, prostate, penile, and testicular cancers through novel mechanisms and refined personalization. This systematic review...

title: Emerging liquid biopsy markers in thyroid cancer: from isolation technologies to clinical translation.
snippet: Diagnosis and treatment of thyroid cancer (TC) are undergoing radical changes, shifting from traditional tissue studies to liquid biopsies (LB). LB technologies provide effective, non-invasive solutions to these challenges. This paper reviews recent advances in the analysis of three important biomarker systems: circulating tumor cells (CTCs), circulating tu...

title: Treatment of left ventricular dysfunction during cancer therapy: a comprehensive review.
snippet: The growing population of cancer survivors has brought to light the critical challenge of cancer therapy-related cardiac dysfunction, particularly with the use of anthracyclines, HER2-targeted agents and other novel therapeutics. This review aims to support the personalized integration of cardiovascular risk management into oncology care to optimize long-te...

title: Robust CD8T cell responses induced by an mRNA-LNP vaccine encoding rat HER2 extracellular domain confer prophylactic tumor protection.
snippet: HER2-positive cancers present challenges of drug resistance, toxicity, and immune tolerance. HER2-targeted peptide vaccines have failed clinically, while nucleoside-modified mRNA-lipid nanoparticle (mRNA-LNP) vaccines show promise for robust antitumor immunity. This study utilized heterologous immunity (rat HER2 extracellular domain, rHER2 ECD) to circumven...

title: TRIM25-mediated ferroptosis resistance is closely associated with poor prognosis in hepatocellular carcinoma.
snippet: TRIM25 has been reported to promote hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) cell survival by activating the Keap1-Nrf2 pathway. However, its expression profile in clinical HCC specimens and its potential role in regulating ferroptosis remain to be elucidated. This study aimed to determine the expression pattern of TRIM25 in primary HCC and its association with clini...

title: Blood cytokine levels in anti-NMDAR and viral encephalitis during the early stage of hospitalization: a small-sample exploratory pilot study.
snippet: We aimed to perform a preliminary analysis of early cytokine profiles in blood between patients with anti-N-methyl-D-aspartate receptor (anti-NMDAR) encephalitis and viral encephalitis (VE), and to observe potential preliminary trends. This study adopted a retrospective design. Following a standardized screening process, anti-NMDAR encephalitis (n = 15), VE...
